channel.title: Pranay's Book Podcast
channel.description: Private feed: 45-minute two-host conversations on books I want to consume on commute.
channel.itunes:author: Pranay Kotasthane

item.title: Focus: The ASML Way — Marc Hijink
item.description: Hijink's inside history of ASML, the Dutch company that built the most complex machine ever made and now sits at the center of the US-China tech war. A monologue walk-through of the EUV bet, the TSMC marriage, the Trump-Biden export rules, and what an Indian semiconductor strategist should take away. Voiced via Sarvam.

item.title: The Gifts of Athena — Joel Mokyr
item.description: Mokyr's argument that the modern knowledge economy was born when 18th-century Europe began bridging propositional knowledge (knowing why) with prescriptive knowledge (knowing how). A two-host walk through useful knowledge, the Industrial Enlightenment, the rise of the factory, the household mortality decline, and Cardwell's Law on why innovation usually loses.

item.title: The Web Beneath the Waves — Samanth Subramanian
item.description: Subramanian's reporting on the 870,000 miles of fiber optic cable that carry 95% of the world's internet, the small clubby industry that maintains them, and how they have become the central front of US-China conflict. Voiced via Sarvam.
